name: Critical Security Tool Killed (Unix)
notes:
This rule triggers when a critical security process has been killed. An adversary may disable security tools to avoid detection. 

Note: The building blocks included in this rule use the reference set Critical Security Tool Processes.
The reference set Critical Security Tool Processes has been pre-populated with common security tool process names. Tune the reference set with any tool used by the organization.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical Security Tool Process Information
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Process Killed match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom) and different QID in 1 minutes
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical Security Tool Process Information, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Process Killed match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom) and different QID in 1 minutes
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical Security Tool Process Information, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Process Killed match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom) and different QID in 1 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Critical Security Tool Killed (Unix) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical Security Tool Process Information, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Process Killed match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom) and different QID in 1 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Critical Security Tool Stopped
Event description: Detects when a critical security tool process has been stopped.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
